project_title: Caregivers’ Insight on Play-Based Activities to Enhance Social Communication Skills in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
abstract:
This proposal examined caregivers' perceptions of using play-based interventions to enable social communication development in children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). It filled an important gap in research regarding how caregiver views can contribute to developing effective interventions and their application. It defined the purpose, research inquiries, and overall goals of the study, as well as a theoretical framework derived from interpretivism and social constructivism. It provided a comprehensive literature review of empirical and theoretical studies to provide a basis for the research's purposes. Using an interpretivist approach for the case study, the research context, participant sampling, data sources, data collection methods, and data analysis methods are explained systematically and justified. Research design issues, ethical considerations, and addressing researcher subjectivity are addressed to provide credibility, dependability, and generalizability.

Keywords: caregiver opinions, play-based interventions, development of social communication, autism spectrum disorder, qualitative research.
